---
title: "Xorg Memory Usage"
date: 2011-03-03
forum: General Help
---

### Post by Craige on 2011-03-03
*In my searches, I came across [this topic]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=494039") that indicated that high xorg memory usage isn't abnormal. However, if the 90MB that this user indicated is "high", then my memory usage is astronomical.*
--------------------------------

I've been noticing that Xorg's memory usage seems to be sitting abnormally high. Currently, it is **using 325MB of ram**. This doesn't seem right to me.

As per the thread I found before, here is a pmap dump of my Xorg process:

```

1406:   /usr/bin/X :0 -nr -verbose -auth /var/run/gdm/auth-for-gdm-574Sv7/database -nolisten tcp vt7
```

### Post by ktaragorn on 2011-05-22
This problem happens to me too. I use Wine to Play world of warcraft, and i think one affects the other.. Over a day of starting and closing Wow maybe 2-5 times Xorg memory goes up from 45M at login, to abt 700-800, and at this point comp slowdown is obvious because my total memory is 3gb and starting wow and video player together becomes hard at this point.

---
